steps.title: Identify why the speaker can't remember
steps.content: The strongest signal is “So blind was I to see and to foresee” — the speaker admits she didn't recognize the moment's significance when it occurred. The metaphor of a tree “that would not blossom yet for many a May” shows the relationship became important only later, meaning she had no reason to pay attention or remember it at the time.

steps.title: Match this logic to the choices
steps.content:
Choice B describes exactly this: the speaker didn't anticipate the relationship's importance, so she couldn't recall its beginning — and she regrets this loss. The “I wish I could remember” opening emphasizes the regret. The other choices either flip the emotion (A), invent details (C), or misdiagnose the problem (D).

That matches choice B — B is correct.
